original: First known interstellar meteor to ever hit Earth - CNEOS 2014-01-08 crash lands in Papua New Guinea
German astronomer Simon Marius independently discovers the first four moons of Jupiter one day afterGalileo
The Great Frost: in London the Thames river froze so thickly bonfires were lit on it

"The 3:16 Game" AFC Wild Card Playof, underdogs Denver Broncos defeat Pittsburgh Steelers 29–23 with Tim Tebow connecting with Demaryius Thomas on an 80-yard touchdown pass on first scrimmage play of overtime
Furman sets NCAA basketball single-game scoring record with 154 pt
Spanish Armada headed by the San José and loaded with gold, sinks after a British squadron attacks off the coast of Colombia (rediscovered in 2015)
Wild Boy of Aveyron (approx.12) discovered in southern France after possibly 7 years in the wild, later christened Victor of Aveyron

Michael Jordan's game-high 35 points leads Chicago to a 120-95 win over Milwaukee, giving him exactly 20,000 points in the 620th game of his NBA career, the second-fastest to reach this milestone after Wilt Chamberlain (499)
Russian manned space craft TM-18, launches into orbit
Rintje Ritsma skates world record 1500m (1:51.60)
"Music City Miracle", in AFC Wild Card Playoff, Tennessee Titans defeat Buffalo Bills 22-16 in last 16 seconds with Kevin Dyson, running length of the field for a game-winning touchdown
Battle of New Orleans (War of 1812); the war had ended on 24th December 1814 but none of the combatants knew
Japan launches Sakigake space probe to Halley's Comet
Jacques Cousteau's first undersea special airs on US network TV
130 wildfires across Australia’s east coast force thousands to evacuate their homes
India all out 63 in one-day international v Australia
TV series "All Creatures Great and Small" debuts on BBC TV, based on the books byJames Herriot, starring Robert Hardy and Christopher Timothy
Frank Watson Dyson, English astronomer, proved Einstein right about light bent by gravity, born in Measham, England (d. 1939)
Comet C/1760 A1 (Great comet) approaches within 0.0682 AUs of Earth
USSR launches Luna 21 for Moon landing
NCAA decides to sponsor women's championships in 5 sports
University of Kentucky Wildcats men's basketball team sets an NCAA record by holding Arkansas State to 6 points in a 75-6 win
William Hartnell, English actor (First Doctor in "Doctor Who"; 134 episodes), born in London (d. 1975)
Harvey Milkbecomes the 1st openly gay person elected to public office in California

Dwight Clark, American football wide receiver ("The Catch" 1981 Super Bowl SF 49ers; First team All-Pro 1982; 2 × Pro Bowl) and executive (SF 49ers, Cleveland Browns), born in Kingston, North Carolina (d. 2018)
Archie Roach, Aboriginal Australian blues singer-songwriter (Took The Children Away), born in the Framlingham Aboriginal Reserve, Australia (d. 2022)
Galina Ulanova, Russian ballet dancer considered one of the greatest ballerinas of the 20th century (Bolshoi Theatre), born in St Petersburg, Russia (d. 1998)
David Lang, American composer (The Little Match Girl Passion - Pulitzer Prize for Music 2008), born in Los Angeles, California[1]
ExplorerWilliam Clarkviews skeleton of a 105 ft whale washed up on Cannon Beach, inhabited by the Tillamook Nation (modern Oregon)

US Army veteran Henry "Robby" Robinson (98) is awarded France's Medal of Honor for his service in WWII by French Ambassador to the US in a ceremony in Las Vegas, Nevada; Robinson worked as a scout behind enemy lines, fought in the Battle of the Bulge, participated in the liberation of the Mauthausen and Gusen concentration camps, and served as a guard during the Nuremberg Trials
RMS Queen Mary 2, the largest passenger ship ever built, is christened by her namesake's granddaughter,Queen Elizabeth II
Vulcan, NASA's first commercially financed moon mission launches from Cape Canaveral, Florida but its lander Peregrine develops problems soon after
Electronics brand LG unveils their Signature OLED T, the world's first wireless TV with a transparent screen at the Consumer Electronics Show in Las Vegas
Sophie Germainis the first woman to win a prize from the Paris Academy of Sciences for her paper on elasticity
African American men granted the right to vote in Washington, D.C. despite PresidentAndrew Johnson's veto
-50°F (-45.6°C), San Jacinto, Nevada (state record)
Storm Filomena hits Spain with 50cm (20 inches) of snow falling on Madrid, resulting in four deaths and leaving thousands stranded
Isabel Allendebegins a letter to her dying grandfather that will become her first novel "The House of the Spirits"
Philip Astley, English 'Father of the Modern Circus', born in Newcastle-under-Lyme, England (d. 1814)
Su Shi [Zizhan], Chinese poet, artist and important figure in Song Dynasty politics, born in Meishan, Sichuan, Northern Song Dynasty (d. 1101)
Johannes Fabricius, Danish astronomer (discovered sunspots), born in East Frisia, Germany (d. 1616)
A magnitude 6.9 earthquake with its epicenter just off the Greek island of Kythira hits much of the country and is felt throughout the entire eastern Mediterranean Sea.
British rock bands The Who and The Kinks perform on the last "Shindig" TV show on ABC
The Beatles' "Rubber Soul" album goes to #1 and stays #1 for six weeks, while the single "We Can Work It Out" also charts at #1 for three weeks
First subway tunnel connecting Manhattan with Brooklyn under the East River opens in New York City
Robert Rodriguez's "El Mariachi" debuts at the Toronto Film Fetsival - will become lowest-budgeted film ever to gross $1 million
Philadelphia Phillies 12-time All Star third basemanMike Schmidtis elected to the Baseball Hall of Fame
"Fearless" Nadia [Mary Ann Evans], Australian stuntwoman and actress (Hunterwali), born in Perth, Western Australia (d. 1996)
Helmuth Hübener, German anti-Nazi activist and pamphleteer, born in Hamburg, Weimar Republic Germany (d. 1942)
Emily Greene Balch, American sociologist, feminist and pacifist (Women's International League for Peace and Freedom - Nobel Peace Prize 1946), born in Boston, Massachusetts (d. 1961)
Robby Krieger, American guitarist (The Doors - "Light My Fire"; "Hello, I Love You"), born in Los Angeles, California
Battle of Ashdown: Ethelred I of Wessex and his brotherAlfred the Greatbeat invading Danish army
A 6.2 magnitude earthquake hit Costa Rica´s region of Volcan Poás, with an epicenter near Cinchona. It was caused by Varablanca-Angel fault.
United Cup Tennis, Ken Rosewall Arena, Sydney, Australia: US wins inaugural event, 4-0 over Italy
Voyageurs National Park, Minn, established
Dmitri Shostakovich' 15th Symphony premieres in Moscow
Vyacheslav Dmitriyevich Zudov, Soviet cosmonaut (Soyuz 23), born in Bor, Russia
Ron Ellis, Canadian ice hockey right wing (Summit Series 1972; Stanley Cup 1967, 4 x NHL All Star; Toronto Maple Leafs), born in Lindsay, Ontario (d. 2024)
Little Anthony [Gourdine], American rhythm and blues singer (Little Anthony and the Imperials - "Goin' Out of My Head"), born in Brooklyn, New York City
Terry Brooks, American fantasy fiction author (Sword of Shannara), born in Sterling, Illinois
